on_error_goto.pattern:
On Error GoTo ErrorHandler

' Code that might fail
result = 10 / value
Debug.Print "Success"
Exit Sub

ErrorHandler:
    MsgBox "Error: " & Err.Description
    Resume Next
on_error_goto.description: On Error GoTo converted to try-catch block

on_error_resume_next.pattern:
On Error Resume Next

' Code that might fail
result1 = operation1()
result2 = operation2()
result3 = operation3()

If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    MsgBox "An error occurred: " & Err.Description
    Err.Clear
End If

On Error GoTo 0
on_error_resume_next.description: On Error Resume Next pattern with individual try-catch blocks

err_raise.pattern:
If value < 0 Then
    Err.Raise 1001, "MyFunction", "Value cannot be negative"
End If

If IsEmpty(data) Then
    Err.Raise vbObjectError + 1002, , "Data is required"
End If
err_raise.description: Err.Raise converted to appropriate exception types

err_object_properties.pattern:
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    Debug.Print "Error Number: " & Err.Number
    Debug.Print "Error Description: " & Err.Description
    Debug.Print "Error Source: " & Err.Source
    Err.Clear
End If
err_object_properties.description: VB6 Err object properties mapped to Exception properties

custom_error_class.pattern:
' In VB6 class module
Public Property Get Number() As Long
    Number = m_Number
End Property

Public Property Let Number(value As Long)
    m_Number = value
End Property

Public Sub RaiseError(errNum As Long, errDesc As String)
    Err.Raise errNum, , errDesc
End Sub
custom_error_class.description: Custom error handling class converted to custom exception

error_logging.pattern:
Private Sub LogError(errNum As Long, errDesc As String, procName As String)
    Open "error.log" For Append As #1
    Print #1, Format(Now, "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss") & " - Error " & errNum & " in " & procName & ": " & errDesc
    Close #1
End Sub
error_logging.description: Error logging to file or using logging framework

validation_with_errors.pattern:
Private Function ValidateInput(value As String) As Boolean
    On Error GoTo ValidationError
    
    If Len(value) = 0 Then
        Err.Raise 1001, , "Value is required"
    End If
    
    If Not IsNumeric(value) Then
        Err.Raise 1002, , "Value must be numeric"
    End If
    
    ValidateInput = True
    Exit Function
    
ValidationError:
    ValidateInput = False
End Function
validation_with_errors.description: Input validation with error handling patterns

finally_equivalent.pattern:
' VB6 doesn't have Finally, but cleanup was done like this:
On Error GoTo ErrorHandler

' Main code
Set obj = CreateObject("Excel.Application")
' Use object
Set obj = Nothing  ' Cleanup
Exit Sub

ErrorHandler:
    If Not obj Is Nothing Then
        Set obj = Nothing  ' Cleanup in error case too
    End If
    Resume Next
finally_equivalent.description: VB6 cleanup patterns converted to try-finally or using statements

nested_error_handling.pattern:
Private Sub MainOperation()
    On Error GoTo MainError
    
    Call SubOperation1
    Call SubOperation2
    Exit Sub
    
MainError:
    MsgBox "Main operation failed: " & Err.Description
End Sub

Private Sub SubOperation1()
    On Error GoTo SubError
    
    ' Risky operation
    Exit Sub
    
SubError:
    Err.Raise Err.Number, "SubOperation1", Err.Description
End Sub
nested_error_handling.description: Nested error handling with exception propagation

error_handling_in_loops.pattern:
For i = 1 To 10
    On Error Resume Next
    result = ProcessItem(i)
    If Err.Number <> 0 Then
        Debug.Print "Failed to process item " & i & ": " & Err.Description
        Err.Clear
    Else
        Debug.Print "Processed item " & i & " successfully"
    End If
Next i
error_handling_in_loops.description: Error handling within loops to continue processing
